regime_fall.current_status: Iran faces existential pressure from simultaneous U.S.-led military escalation (carrier blockade, shoot-on-sight orders, munitions deployment), Israeli strikes degrading military infrastructure, and internal collapse (record political instability, mass protests, security force defections). The regime attempts diplomatic off-ramps through Pakistan while rejecting U.S. ultimatums, but maintains approximately 50% ballistic missile capacity and contested control of the Strait of Hormuz.
regime_fall.last_major_event: On April 26, 2026, President Trump issued an ultimatum demanding Iran's 'surrender with no deals,' the Pentagon deployed additional forces enforcing a Strait of Hormuz blockade with shoot-on-sight authorization against Iranian vessels, Iran's navy briefly shut down the strait vowing continued closure, and Iranian officials rejected negotiations under pressure—marking comprehensive diplomatic and military escalation across all conflict dimensions.
